seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with CrewAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with CrewAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with CrewAI enables multi-agent collaboration for code generation, debugging, testing, and validation in a repository-aware environment. Guardrails are essential to prevent agent delegation loops, hallucinated tool outputs, task drift, and inconsistent code quality that could introduce security vulnerabilities or broken dependencies. Vex integration ensures reliable outputs through automated verification at each task step.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with CrewAI
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Coding Assistant uses CrewAI to orchestrate specialized agents: a Code Analyst reviews repository context and requirements, a Code Generator produces implementation, a Tester executes in sandbox and validates, and a Reviewer performs security and compliance checks. Agents communicate via shared memory and tools, iterating until code passes all validations. This pipeline handles complex coding tasks like feature implementation or bug fixes.

Repository context is injected via tools that parse git repos, while sandbox execution prevents malicious code from escaping. Vex guardrails verify code correctness, security, credential safety, and compliance at each handoff, breaking loops and ensuring consistent quality across the crew.

risks.name: Agent delegation loops
risks.description: Agents endlessly pass incomplete code between roles without converging on a solution
risks.mitigation: Vex task completion detector breaks loops after 3 cycles

risks.name: Hallucinated tool outputs
risks.description: Code Generator fabricates sandbox test results that never actually ran
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies tool outputs match expected schemas and execution logs

risks.name: Task drift
risks.description: Reviewer reinterprets 'fix security bug' as 'add more features' during iteration
risks.mitigation: Vex objective alignment checks ensure task fidelity across agents

risks.name: Inconsistent output quality
risks.description: Tester passes broken code while Reviewer flags same issues, causing crew disagreement
risks.mitigation: Vex standardizes verification criteria across all agent roles

implementation.title: CrewAI Agents Setup
implementation.description: Define specialized agents for the coding pipeline with repo tools and sandbox execution
implementation.code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from vex import Vex
from crewai_tools import GitRepoTool, CodeInterpreterTool

# Code Analyst
analyst = Agent(
    role='Code Analyst',
    goal='Analyze repository context and extract precise requirements',
    backstory='Expert in code review and requirements engineering',
    tools=[GitRepoTool()],
    verbose=True
)

# Code Generator
generator = Agent(
    role='Code Generator',
    goal='Write clean, secure, testable code implementations',
    backstory='10x senior software engineer',
    tools=[CodeInterpreterTool()],
    verbose=True
)

# Tester
tester = Agent(
    role='Quality Assurance Tester',
    goal='Execute code in sandbox and validate against test suite',
    backstory='Test automation specialist',
    tools=[CodeInterpreterTool()],
    verbose=True
)

implementation.title: Core Coding Tasks
implementation.description: Define sequential tasks with clear delegation and Vex verification callbacks
implementation.code:
def vex_code_verify(context):
    return Vex.verify({
        'policy': 'code-assistant',
        'checks': ['code_correctness', 'security_scan'],
        'artifacts': context.get('code', ''),
        'timeout': 30
    })

analyze_task = Task(
    description='Analyze {repo_url} for bug {bug_id}. Extract requirements.',
    agent=analyst,
    context={'repo_url': 'github.com/user/project', 'bug_id': '123'},
    callback=vex_code_verify
)

generate_task = Task(
    description='Implement fix based on analysis: {analysis}. Output only code.',
    agent=generator,
    context={'analysis': analyze_task},
    callback=vex_code_verify
)

test_task = Task(
    description='Test {code} in sandbox. Run test suite. Report failures.',
    agent=tester,
    context={'code': generate_task},
    callback=vex_code_verify
)

implementation.title: Crew Orchestration
implementation.description: Assemble crew with memory and execute with Vex monitoring
implementation.code:
crew = Crew(
    agents=[analyst, generator, tester],
    tasks=[analyze_task, generate_task, test_task],
    verbose=2,
    memory=True,
    max_rpm=100,
    share_crew=False
)

# Execute with Vex monitoring
result = crew.kickoff(
    inputs={
        'repo_url': 'https://github.com/user/project',
        'bug_id': '123',
        'test_suite': 'tests/test_bug123.py'
    },
    vex_config={
        'policy': 'coding-assistant',
        'max_iterations': 5,
        'loop_detection': True
    }
)

print('Verified Code:', result)

implementation.title: Security & Compliance Tools
implementation.description: Custom tools for vulnerability scanning and license checking integrated with Vex
implementation.code:
from crewai_tools import BaseTool

import bandit
class SecurityScanner(BaseTool):
    name: str = "Security Scanner"
    description: str = "Scans Python code for security vulnerabilities"

    def _run(self, code: str) -> str:
        with open('temp.py', 'w') as f:
            f.write(code)
        result = bandit.BanditResult.from_bandin(bandit.cli.main(['temp.py']))
        Vex.verify({
            'policy': 'security-scan',
            'scan_results': result.as_json(),
            'threshold': 'medium'
        })
        return f"Security score: {result.confidence}"

scanner = SecurityScanner()
reviewer = Agent(
    role='Security Reviewer',
    goal='Scan code for vulnerabilities and compliance',
    tools=[scanner],
    verbose=True
)
